commands.review_summary:
The branch centralizes OpenAI Responses tool conversion through the shared provider converter and skips unreadable tool descriptors or schemas while preserving healthy tools, deterministic ordering, and strict-mode shaping.

PR surface: Source +107, Tests +172. Total +279 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The current converter reads and normalizes every Responses tool without a guard, and the PR’s focused tests show throwing descriptors, revoked schema proxies, and cyclic schemas as source-level repro paths.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the reviewed patch has no actionable code finding; maintainers should handle draft status, CI, and merge readiness.

target.title: fix(openai): skip unreadable responses tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- Skip unreadable OpenAI Responses tool descriptors and schemas during request conversion instead of letting one bad tool crash the turn.
- Reuse the shared Responses converter from the OpenClaw transport request builder so the provider and transport paths share the same projection behavior.
- Preserve healthy sibling tools, deterministic sorting, and native strict-mode shaping when broken siblings are skipped.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-tools.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-tools.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- Remote proof attempted with `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 5m --ttl 20m --timing-json --shell -- "pwd >/dev/null"`; blocked by coordinator 401 on `/v1/runs` and `/v1/leases`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A hostile or broken OpenAI Responses tool schema can no longer abort request construction before a model turn; unreadable tools are skipped and healthy tools remain available.

Real environment tested: Local linked OpenClaw worktree using the repo Vitest wrapper against the shared provider converter and OpenClaw transport request builder.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`

Evidence after fix: The focused tests cover throwing descriptor getters, revoked schema proxies, cyclic schemas, shared schema objects, and the OpenClaw transport `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` path.

Observed result after fix: `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` passed 10 tests and `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` passed 241 tests.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` and remote Crabbox/Testbox proof were not completed because Blacksmith is not authenticated locally and AWS Crabbox coordinator calls returned 401. No live OpenAI API call was made; this patch is request-construction behavior.
target.author: vincentkoc
